calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Continued To Mar-22-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Set For Trial On Sep-21-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chad A. Westfall, Esq. of MUSICK PEELER & GARRETT LLP, T:415.281.2030, for defendant & moving party;
Eric K. Larson, Esq. of PILLSBURY & COLEMAN LLP, T:415.433.8000, for plaintiff & opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Landmark American Insurance Company's demurrer to plaintiff Gregory H. Kuhl's complaint is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to support a bad faith breach of the duty to defend by defendant by alleging that (1) defendant failed to accept a settlement offer within its policy limits that would have released all insureds; and (2) by accepting an offer that exhausted defendant's policy limits in exchange of a release of plaintiff's co-insured directors but not plaintiff. An insurer who favors the interests of one insured over those of the other violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (Shell Oil Co. v. National Union Fire Ins. Co. (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1633, 1647 ["National's disbursement of its entire policy limits to indemnify S.I.P. did not discharge National's policy obligations to Shell but rather constituted an actionable breach of those duties"]; Lehto v. Allstate Ins. Co. (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 60, 72 ["An insurer owes the duty of good faith and fair dealing to each of its insureds, and cannot favor the interests of one insured over the other. [Citation.] Moreover, an insurer can breach its duty to its insureds by disbursing the policy proceeds to the insureds' claimant without first obtaining a release of the insureds."]; see also Schwartz v. State Farm Fire and Cas. Co. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 1329, 1339 ["even an insurer that pays the full limits of its policy may be liable for breach of the implied covenant, if improper claims handling causes detriment to the insured"].) Plaintiff has also alleged he has incurred economic loss proximately caused by defendant's breach: the payment of attorney's fees to the insurance coverage counsel he retained after another insurer undertook his defense under a reservation of rights. (Complaint, 9.) Unlike in Emerald Bay Community Assn. v. Golden Eagle Ins. Corp. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1078, where plaintiff was provided a defense and full coverage for the claim by its insurers and thus was "fully protected from both the expense of litigation and the exposure to liability in that lawsuit" (id. at 1094, 1096), here plaintiff alleges that not all of his expenses incurred in defending the action brought by the trustee are covered by defendant or the other insurer.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chad A. Westfall, Esq. of MUSICK PEELER & GARRETT LLP, T:415.281.2030, for defendant & moving party;
Eric K. Larson, Esq. of PILLSBURY & COLEMAN LLP, T:415.433.8000, for plaintiff & opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT LANDMARK AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Landmark American Insurance Company's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Gregory H. Kuhl's complaint is denied. The complaint's allegations support a bad faith breach of the duty to defend that render Brandt fees recoverable. Pleading the ultimate facts of intentional wrongdoing coupled with pleading of the statutory requirements for punitive damages suffices to support a claim for punitive damages. (Perkins v. Superior Court (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 7.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
